MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Feb-04-2019 Set For Trial On Mar-02-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend And File Its First Amended Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-11-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT APPLIED UNDERWRITERS CAPTIVE RISK, CALIFORNIA INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion For Leave To Amend And File Its First Amended Cross-Complaint.

Defendants Applied Underwriters, Inc., Applied Underwriters Captive Risk Assurance Company, Inc. and California Insurance Company's motion for leave to amend and file its first amended cross-complaint is granted. Cross-Defendants may raise any challenge to whether the amended cross-complaint states a cause of action for alter ego liability by way of demurrer, motion for judgment on the pleadings or summary judgment/adjudication. (See Brown v. Ralphs Grocery Co. (2018) 28 Cal.App.5th 824, 843 ["The 'better course of action' is to allow a plaintiff to amend the complaint 'and then let the parties test its legal sufficiency in other appropriate proceedings.' [Citation.] Thus, a defendant is not required to make its arguments about the sufficiency of the pleading in an opposition to a motion for leave to amend."].)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-16-2019 Set For Trial On Aug-12-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Jan-30-2019 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-03-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Larry Lichtenegger, Esq. 831-601-1900 for Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant SAN FRANCISCO INDEPENDENT TAXI ASSOCIATION through Court Call.
Travis Wall, Esq. 415-362-6000 for Defendant/Cross-Complainant Applied Underwriters.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares it's views. Defendant argues the Nielsen decision. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO INDEPENDENT TAXI ASSOCIATION DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.Cross-defendant San Francisco Independent Taxi Association's demurrer to all three causes of action in the cross-complaint is sustained without leave to amend as to the first cause of action for breach of contract by Applied Underwriters Captive Risk Assurance Company, Inc. and overruled as to the second and third causes of action for breach of contract and quasi-contract by California Insurance Company. The Reinsurance Participation Agreement is illegal and unenforceable as a matter of law because it is a collateral agreement that modifies the obligations of the insured and was not filed with the California Department of Insurance as required by Insurance Code section 11658 and 10 CCR 2268(b)). While Nielsen Contracting v. Applied Underwriters, Inc. (2018) 22 Cal.App.5th 1096 did not reach the issue of the RPA's validity beyond the delegation clause and the arbitration provision, the logic of that decision extends to the entire RPA. Although the Insurance Commissioner's determinations in Matter of Shasta Linen Supply, Inc. are not binding, that case provides persuasive reasoning that the RPA is unenforceable as a matter of law. The two Shasta Linen decisions by Judge Shubb held that the rates in the RPA are not invalid per Insurance Code sections 11735 and 11737 because the Insurance Commissioner had not expressly disapproved the rates. Judge Shubb's decisions did not express any view regarding the validity of the RPA per section 11658. Because the RPA deviates from the CIC policies in many respects not just limited to rates, the RPA is an impermissible collateral agreement that is unenforceable in its entirety. CIC sufficiently alleged both a breach of contract claim under the policies and an alternative quasi-contract claim in the event that the contracts that form the bases of the breach of contract claim are determined to be unenforceable.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-12-2018 Continued To Jan-03-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Larry J. Lichtenegger, Esq., Telephone 831-601-1900, appearing for Plaintiff by CourtCall;
Travis R. Wall, Esq., Hinshaw Law, Telephone 415-362-6000, appearing for Defendant, Applied Underwiters;

Ruling - Argued. The Court makes the following ruling:

Cross-Defendant San Francisco Independent Taxi Association's demurrer to all three causes of action in the cross-complaint is continued to January 3, 2019 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 302. Further supplemental briefs to be filed by December 21, 2018 and is limited to whether the claim on unpaid monies due under the RPA by AUCRA has been rendered illegal by the Insurance Commission.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: torreanom@aol.com; (302/HEK)

Notice Of Joinder In Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay The Action
Off Calendar Jan-19-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Larry J. Lichtenegger of the law offices of Larry J. Lichtenegger (831.626.2801) appears via telephone as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Travis R. Wall of the law offices of Hinshaw Culbertson LLP (415.362.6000) appears as counsel for plaintiff/defendant/moving/opposing party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court vacates the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS APPLIED UNDERWRITERS, INC., CALIFORNIA INSURANCE COMPANY Joinder In Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay The Action is set aside, OFF CALENDAR without prejudice to re-setting at a future date. The Court orders the entire action stayed, pending a decision by the Court of Appeal, First Appellate Disctrict. Counsel for defendant shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim. The parties shall re-submit all papers is the motion is re-noticed for hearing.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)
